title: Project Miner Local PM Intake Handoff Guide Disclosure Controls
objective: Reduce day-to-day PM workbench scan burden by wrapping only the existing /pm-review/import-intake Local PM Intake Handoff Guide panel in a browser-native, default-open disclosure control while preserving its placement inside Daily Action Panels, five derived items, labels, hrefs, order, status pills, dynamic text, no-storage behavior, no-authority wording, read seam, and no-authority boundary.
active_role: Technical Repo Authority / PM Product Coordinator / AI Orchestration Usability Implementer
implemented_scope: Convert the existing Local PM Intake Handoff Guide panel wrapper to a native default-open disclosure control.
Preserve the existing pm-handoff-guide id and Local PM intake handoff guide aria label.
Keep the guide inside Daily Action Panels.
Preserve the guide's five derived items, labels, hrefs, order, status pills, dynamic text, no-storage behavior, and no-authority wording.
Preserve existing storage behavior, read seams, route links, quick-jump rail, output actions, output statuses, helper disclosures, and detail disclosures.

Add focused smoke coverage proving default-open state, collapse/reopen behavior, hidden/visible guide items, unchanged link counts and href targets, absence of handoff-guide disclosure-state localStorage keys, and continued PM Lane 083 authority guard posture.
Update PM lane operating docs, project status, packet, and handoff.
sidecar_result: A read-only sidecar from PM Lane 089 recommended PM Lane 090 as Local PM Intake Handoff Guide Disclosure Controls after the route-link disclosure pattern landed. The recommendation was accepted as a browser-local AI/orchestration ergonomics slice. A separate read-only sidecar completed a PM Lane 091 scout and recommended Local PM Intake Workflow Map Disclosure Controls as the next non-overlapping lane, with no write ownership in this tranche.

Modify focused operations-web smoke tests for default-open handoff-guide disclosure controls and no persisted collapse state.
Update PM lane operating docs, project status, packet, and handoff.
Run focused local validation, commit, push, and restore host parity.
not_allowed: No DNS, auth, ingress, or secret change.
